subject: Project Management
title: Chernobyl's New Safe Confinement
description: Learn Chernobyl's New Safe Confinement in one minute.

hook.text: In 2016, the world's largest movable structure slid over Chernobyl's destroyed reactor.

buildup.text: The original concrete sarcophagus from 1986 was crumbling. A new solution was desperately needed.

discovery.text: Engineers built a 36,000-ton steel arch 300 meters away, then slid it into place on Teflon pads.

twist.text: Workers could only spend minutes near the reactor due to radiation. Everything was built remotely.

climax.text: The New Safe Confinement will contain the radiation for 100 years — a project born from catastrophe.

punchline.text: They built the world's largest lid to seal the world's worst mistake.

quiz.question: How was the New Safe Confinement installed at Chernobyl?
options: Built 300 meters away and slid into place on Teflon pads
Assembled piece by piece directly over the reactor
